commit.message:
AI-300 transformation: Bicep IaC, GitHub Actions, Foundry, evaluation, observability

Complete retooling of Claude.Bricks from DP-100 lab to AI-300 operations platform.

Infrastructure as Code:
- 16 Bicep modules (storage, keyvault, acr, log-analytics, app-insights,
  aml-workspace, aml-compute, cognitive-services, search, foundry-hub,
  foundry-project, aml-registry, rbac, vnet, private-endpoint)
- main.bicep orchestrator with conditional deployment flags
- Dev and test parameter files
- Deployment wrapper scripts (deploy, whatif, teardown, validate)

CI/CD:
- 5 GitHub Actions workflows (infra, train, deploy-model, eval-rag, drift-check)
- OIDC authentication, blue/green deployment, metric-gated quality checks

ML Operations:
- Blue/green endpoint deployment scripts (canary, promote, rollback, smoke test)
- Asset registration scripts (data assets, components, environments, registry)
- Model promotion flow (dev to test via shared AML registry)
- Drift detection with PSI/KS metrics and auto-retrain triggers

GenAI Operations:
- Foundry-native Scenario 4 (create project, deploy model, configure index,
  run evaluation, configure monitoring, trace analysis)
- 15-row evaluation dataset with LEGO domain test cases
- Custom buildability evaluator
- Prompt versioning (system v1/v2, RAG v1/v2) with experiment framework
- RAG tuning matrix runner

Observability:
- Telemetry client wrapping Application Insights SDK
- 5 KQL dashboard queries (latency, tokens, groundedness, errors, failures)
- Drift detection and failure trending scripts

Documentation:
- As-built HTML fully rewritten for AI-300 (17 sections, 5 exam domains)
- Planning docs archived to archive/
- Operational runbooks (endpoint deployment, asset lifecycle)
- Updated .gitignore for eval results, Bicep artifacts, Terraform state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify VarLvalueNonblocking → §10.4.2 [skip ci]

The test performs a nonblocking assignment (`x <= 8'h99`) to a logic variable inside an initial block. IEEE 1800-2023 §10.4.2 defines nonblocking procedural assignments, including their syntax (`variable_lvalue <= expression`) and scheduling semantics (RHS evaluated immediately, LHS updated at end of time step).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add foreach expansion at parse time and make tup parse store to DB (T2)

Foreach rules are now expanded into individual CMD nodes during parsing,
matching C tup's behavior exactly. Each matched input file produces a
separate CMD node with fully substituted command strings.

Key changes:
- expand_rules_for_dir(): expands foreach rules, applies % substitutions
- Input globs now resolve against declared outputs from prior rules
  (needed for *.o matching outputs from foreach *.c rules)
- tup parse: now runs scan + parse + store (phases 1-2), not just printing
- t2000-t2003 all pass (basic rules, foreach, link objects, variables)

commit.message:
fix(agent): correct tool message ordering in streaming path (#88)

The streaming path (stream_with_tool_loop) was adding tool_result
messages to the session before the assistant tool_use message. The
Anthropic API strictly requires assistant(tool_use) → user(tool_result)
ordering and rejects requests with the wrong order.

Collect tool results into a Vec during execution, then add the assistant
message first followed by tool results — matching the non-streaming
handle_response() pattern.

commit.message:
Fix: grade extraction regex was matching wrong anchor tag

The previous regex '>Grade:<[\s\S]*?<a>' was too greedy and matched
anchor tags far away (e.g. languages.un.org link). Now uses a simpler
approach: find "Grade:" text, then look for the first <a> tag within
200 characters — which is always the grade value like "P-4".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify BlockingIntraAssignDelayCapturesRHS → §9.4.5 [skip ci]

The test assigns `a = #5 b` where `b=10` at time 0 and changes to 99 at time 2. The result written to `a` is 10, confirming that the RHS is captured immediately and the LHS write is deferred — the core semantic of intra-assignment timing controls defined in §9.4.5.

commit.message:
Refactor: deduplicate review orchestration, add concurrent LLM calls, fix all clippy warnings

- Extract shared review helpers into state.rs (ReviewEventBuilder, progress callbacks,
  mark_running/complete_review/fail_review, ~200 lines removed from api.rs/github.rs)
- Add concurrent per-file LLM calls via futures::buffer_unordered(5) in pipeline.rs
- Add CliOverrides struct to replace 16-param apply_cli_overrides
- Add WebhookReviewParams struct to fix too_many_arguments in github.rs
- Add Display + as_str() for Severity/Category enums, replace Debug formatting
- Add ReviewListItem for lightweight list endpoint
- Add review_semaphore (tokio::Semaphore) for rate limiting concurrent reviews
- Use url::Url::parse for Ollama port detection instead of string contains
- Use HashSet for O(1) symbol deduplication in extract_symbols_from_diff
- Return JoinHandle from save_reviews_async/save_config_async
- Simplify boolean expression (is_none_or) and fix all map_or → is_some_and
- Fix all dead_code warnings (model_name, resolve_provider, as_str, title)
- 579 tests passing, 0 clippy warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: separate token flow into context/input/output

Token visualization now clearly shows:
- Context: system prompt + tools + history (resent every request)
- User Input: the new user message (typically small)
- Output: LLM generated response (often 2-4x user input)
- Output Ratio: output/input multiplier

This better reflects how LLM APIs work - the full context is sent
with every request, and outputs are typically larger than the
user's input message.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Begin Phase 5: Task breakdown for Slice 3 (Auth & RBAC)

Create comprehensive task breakdown for Slice 3 (006-tasks.auth-rbac.md):
- 32 granular tasks across 6 phases: setup, database, service, API, routes, validation
- Dependency chain: config → exceptions/roles → models → migrations → repository → service → middleware → endpoints
- TDD ordering: implementation immediately followed by tests
- Commit checkpoints throughout (not deferred to end)
- Covers JWT RS256 (15min access/7day refresh), bcrypt (work factor 12)
- RBAC with 8 roles + fine-grained permissions via @require_role/@require_permission decorators
- User/RefreshToken models with RLS enforcement
- 3 auth endpoints: POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, POST /auth/logout
- Test coverage: models, migrations, service, middleware, endpoints, RBAC
- Ready for implementation phase

Phase 5 (Task Breakdown) begins. Slice 1-2 in_progress, Slice 3 tasks now defined.
Phase 6 (Implementation) starts after this PR is approved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify BlockingIntraAssignDelayBlocksFlow → §9.4.5 [skip ci]

The test uses `a = #5 b`, a blocking intra-assignment delay: the RHS `b` is evaluated at time 0, the assignment to `a` is delayed 5 time units, and `c = 8'd99` (which follows sequentially) does not execute until after that delay. This is precisely the semantics defined in §9.4.5 Intra-assignment timing controls.

commit.message:
feat: OTT/Approach attribution correction v1 (#37)

* feat: add OTT/Approach attribution correction layer (V1)

Insert Layer 4.5 in the V3 pipeline that detects FIR/GIR divergence
patterns and redistributes a bounded amount between OTT and Approach.
Zero-sum, capped at ±0.5 strokes, confidence-gated, with kill switch.

Addresses GOL-5/GOL-6 without requiring new user input.

commit.message:
fix banners: lift outside max-w-3xl, use plain img tag

The full-bleed calc(50% - 50vw) trick requires the banner to be a
direct child of a full-width container. Placing it inside a max-w-3xl
div caused the 50% calculation to use 384px instead of the viewport
half-width, offsetting the banner off-screen.

commit.message:
Phase 6: JavaScript template refactoring - eliminate remaining 40 inline styles

✅ Added 60+ CSS classes to app.css covering:
  - Modal image heights
  - Logbook exercise display (rest values, buttons)
  - Weekly/monthly statistics cards styling
  - Empty state messages (standard + compact padding)
  - Bodyfat tracking display components
  - Ranking items with progress bars
  - Meal cards with macros display
  - Quick food button styling
  - Recipe cards with macro display
  - Shopping list items with toggle states
  - Full-width utilities

✅ Refactored app.js templates to use CSS classes instead of inline styles:
  - Modal exercise details (line 424)
  - Program exercise display (lines 556, 562)
  - Logbook empty state (line 1167)
  - Weekly stats display (lines 1296-1310)
  - Monthly stats cards (lines 1351-1371)
  - Friend list empty state (line 1484)
  - Friend action button (line 1493)
  - Bodyfat tracking list (lines 1604-1615)
  - Top exercises ranking (lines 1794-1805)
  - Meal cards display (lines 1906-1917)
  - Quick food buttons (lines 1985-1987)
  - Recipe cards (lines 2061-2074)
  - Shopping list items (lines 2136-2142)
  - Shopping list empty state (line 2131)

Result: Reduced inline styles from 40+ to 1 (only dynamic width % remains, which is necessary)

The refactoring maintains 100% visual fidelity while improving:
- Code maintainability
- CSS reusability
- Design consistency via tokenized classes
- Separation of concerns (styles in CSS, markup in JS)

commit.message:
Begin Phase 5: Task breakdown for Slice 3 (Auth & RBAC)

Create comprehensive task breakdown for Slice 3 (006-tasks.auth-rbac.md):
- 32 granular tasks across 6 phases: setup, database, service, API, routes, validation
- Dependency chain: config → exceptions/roles → models → migrations → repository → service → middleware → endpoints
- TDD ordering: implementation immediately followed by tests
- Commit checkpoints throughout (not deferred to end)
- Covers JWT RS256 (15min access/7day refresh), bcrypt (work factor 12)
- RBAC with 8 roles + fine-grained permissions via @require_role/@require_permission decorators
- User/RefreshToken models with RLS enforcement
- 3 auth endpoints: POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh, POST /auth/logout
- Test coverage: models, migrations, service, middleware, endpoints, RBAC
- Ready for implementation phase

Phase 5 (Task Breakdown) begins. Slice 1-2 in_progress, Slice 3 tasks now defined.
Phase 6 (Implementation) starts after this PR is approved.
